title: Using Glyphs
text: Using Glyphs You can use glyphs in form components and widgets, panels, menus, and drag-and-drop formats. To use a glyph for an operation button in a Panel, select the glyph in the Panel's Glyph Name property. To use a glyph as an icon on a Command Button, Picture, Drag Source, or Drop Target widget: Set the field's Data Type to Image (glyph) The Windows command button does not support images within the command button. If you want image functionality in command buttons, set Representation to Uniface . Specify the name of the glyph in the Initial Value property. If you load a glyph for which there are a number of variations, Uniface attempts to load the one with the most suitable size. Microsoft Windows metafiles are rendered on a bitmap correctly sized before displaying. Optionally, to scale an image to fit a command button, use the Scale and Preserve Aspect properties. To use the glyph as a background image of a Form, Entity, or Windows Application Shell, specify the glyph in the Background Image property or the object. Related Links Create and Maintain Glyphs Glyphs are maintained and deployed in a runtime library. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
